content: Branch naming convention: overstory/{agentName}/{taskId} — required for merge.ts parseAgentName/parseTaskId extraction. Fallback to 'unknown' if doesn't match (no validation error).

Tiered conflict resolution: Tier 1 (clean-merge git merge --no-edit), Tier 2 (auto-resolve keep-incoming with content-loss prevention), Tier 3 (AI-resolve via Claude buildPrintCommand), Tier 4 (reimagine reimplement from scratch). Each tier independently configurable via config.merge.aiResolveEnabled/reimagineEnabled.

Content loss prevention in auto-resolve: hasContentfulCanonical() skips files where canonical side has non-whitespace content in conflict block. Escalates to Tier 3 instead of silently discarding canonical edits. merge=union files bypass check and keep both sides concatenated.

Prose detection in AI tiers: looksLikeProse() detects conversational preambles (I , Here , Let me , I cannot, etc.) and markdown fencing. If Claude wraps code in markdown, escalates to next tier. Prevents invalid/wrapped responses from being committed.

Pre-merge validation: Auto-commits os-eco state files (.overstory/, .seeds/, .mulch/, .canopy/, .claude/, CLAUDE.md) to prevent merge refusal. Stashes remaining dirty tracked files, deletes untracked overlaps. Restores stash in finally block.

Tier skip logic: queryConflictHistory() searches mulch for merge-conflict patterns on files in entry.filesModified. If any tier has failed >= 2 times for overlapping files AND never succeeded, add to skipTiers. Prevents repeated failed attempts at same tier. Mulch unavailability is soft failure (fireandforget).
